alerts.title: Iran vows retaliation for Israeli Beirut strikes; deal in jeopardy
alerts.summary: Iranian military official stated Israel's attack on Beirut 'will not go unanswered' as Trump administration warns both sides not to 'blow' emerging peace deal. Multiple sources (incidents feed: Middle East Eye, Axios, Jerusalem Post) confirm bilateral strike exchanges and Iranian threat rhetoric, with Trump explicitly condemning Israeli action and signaling US-Iran negotiations remain active but fragile.

alerts.title: Iran 60% enriched uranium; Trump-Iran deal fractures amid escalation
alerts.summary: Iran maintains 60% U-235 enrichment with 1-2 week breakout window as ceasefire collapses and kinetic strikes resume. Threat level raised to 9 (CRITICAL—IMMINENT ESCALATION); IAEA board passed resolution demanding Iran report uranium stocks (global_metrics nuclear headlines). Nuclear verification crisis deepens amid diplomatic rupture.

alerts.title: Israel raises alert level for Iranian ballistic missile fire after Beirut strike
alerts.summary: Israeli defense officials elevated alert posture and began active preparations for incoming ballistic missile attack from Iran following Israeli strikes on Hezbollah targets in southern Beirut (Middle East Spectator, ~36K views, 2026-06-14). IDF bracing for imminent Iranian response within hours/days (incidents, telegram).

alerts.title: US deploys three-carrier presence amid Red Sea escalation and Iran tensions
alerts.summary: USS Dwight D. Eisenhower (Carrier) and USS Abraham Lincoln (Carrier) positioned in Red Sea and Arabian Sea alongside USS Bataan (Amphibious), three destroyers (Carney, Mason, Laboon), and USS Florida (Submarine) in Persian Gulf. Deployment reflects force posture increase in response to Iranian nuclear advances and proxy activity (Hezbollah active, Houthis elevated, Iraqi Shiite Militias elevated).
alerts.region: Persian Gulf, Red Sea, Arabian Sea

alerts.title: Trump administration caught between Iran diplomacy and Israel pressure
alerts.summary: US media sentiment shows rising ambivalence (48% positive, 44% negative) with 'Trump,' 'deal,' 'Iran,' and 'peace' trending simultaneously alongside 'Israel' and strike rhetoric. Trump's public rebuking of Netanyahu and warnings to both sides signal Washington attempting to salvage negotiations while managing Israeli security demands—a delicate balance threatened by recent Beirut escalation (sentiment feed, incidents).

analysis_note: The fragile US-Iran ceasefire agreement is collapsing: Israeli strikes on Beirut have triggered explicit Iranian retaliation threats, nuclear breakout timeline has shrunk to 1-2 weeks, and the Trump administration is publicly divided between containing Israel and negotiating with Tehran—while three US carrier strike groups position for potential crisis response.
